summary: Comprehensive testing of Repairs Module completed successfully. Backend APIs verified: 30 repair parts with proper pricing structure, 96 repair rules linking questions to parts, 38 available questions from Q&A categories. Frontend UI verified: Spare Parts tab displays all 30 parts with Hatchback/Sedan/SUV pricing, Question Rules tab displays all 96 rules with linked questions, Add Part and Create Rule modals working correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: Repair rules in database use flat structure (condition_type, condition_value, action_type) while frontend modal expects nested conditions array - both structures are supported
All 30 parts have proper pricing for all 3 car types (hatchback, sedan, suv) with repair_price, replace_price, repair_labor, replace_labor
All 96 rules reference valid parts and have proper condition structures
Available questions endpoint correctly returns 38 unique questions from inspection Q&A categories
seed_data_creation: Sample data pre-populated: 30 repair parts, 13 Q&A categories with 38 questions, 96 repair rules
context_for_next_testing_agent: Repairs Module fully functional. Backend APIs at /api/repair-parts and /api/repair-rules working correctly. Frontend accessible via Settings > Repairs Module tab. Sample data includes parts like AC Compressor, Radiator, Front Bumper, etc. with pricing for all car types.

frontend_tests.test: Add Part modal has pricing inputs for all car types
Create Rule modal has part and question selects

GET /api/repair-parts.structure: Parts have id, name, category, hatchback/sedan/suv pricing objects

GET /api/repair-rules.structure: Rules have id, part_id, question_id, question_text, condition_type, action_type, part enrichment

GET /api/repair-rules/available-question.structure: Questions have question_id, question_text, category_name, question_type, options
